criteria.description: Asks for or infers the correct target (app/dev) and resolves {APP_DIR} / {GITHUB_REPO} / {APPS_EXTRA} before touching files
When dev mode is selected, verifies .github repo presence, active branch, and local-vs-remote state before editing
Loads Sources of Truth from {GITHUB_REPO}/docs/claude/writing-docs.md before the gap analysis
Applies Reference-Don't-Duplicate, Audience, and Writing Anti-Patterns from writing-docs.md during gap analysis
App mode cross-checks docs against ADRs (NL Design, i18n, Security, Screenshots) without inserting ADR links into user-facing docs
Only modifies .md files — never edits schema.yaml/config.yaml/settings.json even when drift is detected there
Presents a summary table and asks for confirmation before writing anything (never auto-saves)
Routes observations through candidates → learnings per the two-stage buffer described in SKILL.md

evals.description: App-mode sync when an implemented feature (per specs) is undocumented in the app's feature docs
evals.setup: {APPS_EXTRA}/openregister/openspec/specs/export/spec.md has status=done. {APPS_EXTRA}/openregister/docs/features/ has no export.md. {APPS_EXTRA}/openregister/README.md lists export as [Future].
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude tends to open the README, rewrite marketing prose, and either miss the spec entirely or duplicate spec content into the feature doc rather than linking.
evals.expected_output: Flags the missing feature doc and stale [Future] marker; proposes a new docs/features/export.md that references the spec (not copies it); updates README feature list; asks for confirmation before writing.
assertions: reads openspec/specs/export/spec.md before editing any doc
flags README [Future] marker as stale (spec status=done)
proposes docs/features/export.md referencing the spec via link, not duplicating content
does NOT link to ADRs from user-facing docs (guardrail: No ADR links in app docs)
uses AskUserQuestion before writing
runs ADR-009 screenshot check on the new feature doc

evals.description: Dev-mode sync when the local .github repo is behind origin — skill must warn before editing
evals.setup: ~/.github exists, on branch main, but local HEAD is 3 commits behind origin/main.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude edits writing-docs.md on the stale branch, producing a diff that will conflict with upstream work already merged.
evals.expected_output: Step 0 Step 3 reports 'local is behind remote'; offers git pull; does NOT apply any edits until the user acknowledges or pulls.
assertions: runs `git -C {GITHUB_REPO} fetch origin main` in Step 0 Step 3
detects and reports the 3-commit drift
offers a concrete remediation (git pull) before editing
blocks the sync from applying edits until divergence is resolved or acknowledged
does not touch any .md files in {GITHUB_REPO} during this run

evals.description: Pre-flight Check B catches a Sources of Truth entry that points to a renamed file
evals.setup: Writing-docs.md Sources of Truth table lists `.claude/openspec/config.yaml` (old path), but the active path is `.claude/openspec/config.yaml` renamed from an earlier draft. ADR-017 exists on disk but is not in the table.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude treats writing-docs.md as authoritative and follows the broken path without verifying file existence.
evals.expected_output: Step 0.5 Check B reports the dangling path and the missing ADR-017 entry. Asks to update writing-docs.md before continuing.
assertions: runs Step 0.5 Check B with file-exists assertions on each Sources of Truth entry
reports the dangling entry with the actual canonical path as replacement suggestion
reports ADR-017 as a candidate addition to the table
asks whether to update writing-docs.md first
applies edits only to .md files

evals.description: Guardrail test: skill must refuse to edit schema.yaml even when Check E / C shows it drifted from writing-specs.md
evals.setup: writing-specs.md uses GIVEN/WHEN/THEN explicitly; openspec/schemas/conduction/schema.yaml still references an older `- When / - Then` shorthand on line 178.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude edits schema.yaml directly since it's clear drift.
evals.expected_output: Check C reports the drift. Does NOT edit schema.yaml. Logs the drift for manual follow-up and updates writing-specs.md if needed (or leaves it as the canonical source).
assertions: reports the writing-specs.md vs schema.yaml drift
refuses to write to openspec/schemas/conduction/schema.yaml (docs-only guardrail)
offers a manual follow-up note in the final report
applies any .md edits only where the user confirms

should_trigger: I need to sync the docs
can you update the app docs
check if the docs are up to date for openregister
update the docs to match the current state
audit the app docs for drift
